data.title: CVC looking for partners for potential ITV takeover - report
data.description:
Private equity firm CVC Capital Partners is evaluating teaming up for a potential ITV Plc (OTCPK:ITVPF) bid.

The PE shop is seeking a partner to split ITV's studio and broadcasting businesses, according to a Dealreporter item on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

CVC has sounded out Sony Corp. (SONY) and MediaforEurope for a potential purchase of ITV (OTCPK:ITVPF), the report said. Sony would likely want the ITV Studios, while MFE would be interested in the broadcasting segment.

Talks were held in recent weeks and deliberations are still at an early stage, according to the report.

data.title: Big Lots 'going out of business' a huge opportunity for Ollie's Bargain Outlet
data.description:
The “Going Out of Business” sale at Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) could be a huge opportunity for Ollie’s Bargain Outlet (NASDAQ:OLLI) to scoop up market share and real estate at bargain prices. Piper Sandler’s Peter Keith sees near-term headwinds to OLLI's comparable sales from Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) close-out sales but expects a “significant” lift in sales in 2025 from a full liquidation.

Keith also sees a benefit to Ollie’s (OLLI) from the real estate left empty by Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ), enabling competitors in an abnormally strong negotiating position for these sites.

“While Ollie shares have rallied 22% since the Q3 print last week, we believe significant upside remains as a Big Lots liquidation would represent the greatest market share and store opportunity for OLLI in its 40+ year history,” Keith writes in Friday’s research note.

The initial drag on OLLI comparable sales will be seen in Q4 and Q1 2025 with roughly 800 OLLI stores impacted, resulting in a headwind of as much as 3% to 4% spread across the two quarters. But on the other end, with ~900 Big Lots (OTC:BIGGQ) closures overlapping with existing OLLI stores, Keith estimates a +7% comparable sales lift to OLLI in 2025 from share capture.

Piper Sandler assigns an Overweight rating to Ollie’s Bargain Outlook (OLLI) and $126 price target, which assumes a 7% upside for the stock.

data.title: Ares Management, Tradeweb upgraded to Outperform at KBW
data.description:
Keefe, Bruyette & Woods upgraded Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) and Tradeweb Markets (NASDAQ:TW) to Outperform as KBW expects a favorable outlook for capital markets in 2025, with an expectation that the sector will generate close to ~10% total returns.

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) stock climbed 2.9%, and Tradeweb (NASDAQ:TW) gained 2.7% in Friday afternoon trading.

For retail brokers, analyst Kyle Voigt raised estimates due to a higher interest rate forecast, signs of growth in sweep cash balances, and a supportive equity market backdrop. For exchanges, KBW said valuations are reasonable, while companies still offer solid downside protection with defensive growth. For alternative asset managers, the firm sees a favorable fundamental outlook and beneficial cyclical factors, such as credit spreads being near 17-year lows.

Voigt sees a higher base rate forecast and resilient economic growth outlook strengthening institutional/retail demand for credit products in 2025, prompting him to upgrade Ares (ARES) from Market Perform.

"We don’t think the recent shift in the yield curve will be enough to derail a return of M&A activity in 2025, and instead continue to expect a sharp rebound in M&A volumes — which should help drive better net deployment trends for ARES in 2025 and should also help to create a better supply/demand balance in the direct lending market more broadly," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

Tradeweb (TW) stands to benefit from higher-for-longer rates boosting rates activity, share gains in the cash Treasury, its partnership with BlackRock's (BLK) Aladdin platform, and strength in emerging markets swaps.

"TW's current FY3 multiple of 35.6X is still a material 18% discount to valuations reached in 2H21 during ZIRP (zero interest rate policy)," Voigt wrote. "Overall, we don't see TW's multiple as over-stretched here,and believe it could hold as long as double-digit organic revenue growth persists into 2026 (which we currently expect with organic revenue growth of ~11%)."

KBW's Outperform rating on Ares (ARES) contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and agrees with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

Similarly, the SA Quant system has a Hold rating on Tradeweb (TW), in contrast to the average Wall Street rating of buy.

data.title: Granite Ridge Resources jumps on report of Northern Oil takeover bid
data.description:
• Granite Ridge Resources (NYSE:GRNT) rose 6% after a report that Northern Oil and Gas (NOG) has made an offer for the shale oil producer.
  • Northern Oil (NOG) has submitted at least two bids for Granite Ridge (GRNT), according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The latest offer was at a 20% premium to GRNT's share price.
  • Developing story ...

data.title: Five reasons why UBS is overweight the UK
data.description:
UBS analysts said they are overweight the United Kingdom (NYSEARCA:EWU) in their Outlook 2025 report.

Andrew Garthwaite, chief global equity strategist at UBS, named five reasons why:

The UK (EWU) outperforms when defensive sectors outperform. Here, UBS analysts explain why they prefer defensives over cyclicals.

Europe (VGK), (IEV), (IEUR) remains “abnormally cheap” on sector-adjusted price-over-earnings. “Nearly all sectors (except for commercial services and luxury) are trading on higher-than-normal discounts,” said Garthwaite. “The buyback yield in the UK (as percentage of market cap) is much higher than the U.S. and it used to be the other way around.”

The UK benefits from a stronger dollar because nearly 20% of revenues come from the U.S. UBS analysts forecasts the dollar to slowly appreciate by end 2026, although not in 2025.

The UK 10-year/Germany 10-year government bond spread “seems to have moved too far,” Garthwaite said. The UK expected inflation rate over 2030-2035 is at 3.4%.

Lastly, “domestic factors look better than elsewhere,” he said. UBS forecasts an above consensus for 2025 UK GDP growth at 1.5% (consensus is at 1.4%). “The UK particularly benefits from strong real wage growth (currently 2.5%, but likely to slow), a pick-up in employment (UBS forecasts 1.4% employment growth in 2025), and fiscal easing (1.1% of GDP).”

Here are top-rated SA Quant stocks from the UK. Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special “Quantamental” analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

  1. Gorilla Technology Group Inc. (GRRR) – Quant rating: 4.98
  2. International Consolidated Airlines Group (OTCPK:ICAGY) – Quant rating: 4.94
  3. British American Tabacco (BTI) – Quant rating: 4.85
  4. Verona Pharma (VRNA) – Quant rating: 4.81
  5. Imperial Brands (OTCQX:IMBBY) – Quant rating: 4.79
  6. Tesco Plc (OTCPK:TSCDY) – Quant rating: 4.78
  7. Janus Henderson Group (JHG) – Quant rating: 4.68
  8. Reckitt Benckiser Group (OTCPK:RBGLY) – Quant rating: 4.44
  9. Centessa Pharmaceuticals (CNTA) – Quant rating: 4.34
  10. Smith Group (OTCPK:SMGZY) – Quant rating: 4.29
  11. Roivant Sciences (ROIV) – Quant rating: 4.27
  12. Unilever Plc (UL) – Quant rating: 3.87

data.title: Texas Instruments finalizes $1.6B CHIPs Act award
data.description:
Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN) announced on Friday that it has finalized the $1.6B award from the Biden administration's $53B CHIPs Act to help it expand its plants in Texas and Utah.

The grants will be used to support three new 300 millimeter semiconductor wafer fabs already under construction, the company said in a statement. Unlike cutting edge chips used for artificial intelligence and other high-end technologies, these are considered "legacy" chips, used in a variety of consumer devices.

Texas Instruments also said it expects to receive between $6B and $8B from the U.S. Treasury's Investment Tax Credit for "qualified U.S. manufacturing investments" and up to $10M for workforce development.

The new plants — which will be located in Sherman, Texas and Lehi, Utah — will create roughly 2,000 new jobs, Texas Instruments said.

The award was first announced in August.

"As the largest analog and embedded processing semiconductor manufacturer in the U.S., TI is uniquely positioned to provide dependable, low-cost 300mm semiconductor manufacturing capacity at scale," said Texas Instruments President and CEO Haviv Ilan. "The increasing number of electronic devices in our lives depend on our foundational chips, and we appreciate the support from the U.S. government to make the semiconductor ecosystem stronger and more resilient."

Shares of the analog chip supplier rose 2% in midday trading.

data.title: Merit Medical gets FDA approval for WRAPSODY dialysis device
data.description:
Merit Medical Systems (NASDAQ:MMSI) has received FDA approval for its WRAPSODY Cell-Impermeable Endoprosthesis device for the treatment of dialysis patients.

The device is designed to extend long-term vessel patency and preserve vascular access in dialysis patients. Merit plans to begin commercializing the product in the U.S. in 2025.

In a note, Needham analyst Mike Matson said the approval came earlier than expected. The investment bank, which has a buy rating on the stock, estimates the U.S. market for the product at $150M to $250M.

"Given WRAPSODY's strong clinical data and potential synergies from MMSI's other dialysis products, we expect WRAPSODY to become a meaningful growth driver and estimate that it could contribute $15-25M of sales and $0.11-0.18 of EPS in 2025E," Matson added.

data.title: UBS says ‘underweight the names with high China exposure’
data.description:
U.S. names with China exposure face weaker growth, wrote Andrew Garthwaite, chief global equity strategist at UBS, in his Outlook 2025 report.

He said that there are three general threats to China-exposed stocks:

  • Weaker growth: about 3.7% in 2025 and 2.7% in 2026.
  • A weaker renminbi (7.60 by end 2025, analysts said)
  • The threat of retaliatory tariffs on Western products.

UBS listed neutral U.S. stocks with China exposure.

“The problem being that this time around, unlike 2018, U.S. tariffs on China look to be much larger and thus harder to offset with currency depreciation (for the renminbi) and are more likely to lead China to seek to redirect its exports elsewhere,” said Garthwaite.

“This could lead say to Europe imposing tariffs on Chinese products in response, which in turn leads to the risk of retaliatory tariffs by China,” he added.

Also, for the consumer names, there is the risk of buying boycotts from China to U.S. or Western products in response to the tariffs, he said.

U.S. companies with high China exposure:

  • Nike (NKE) – China exposure: 15%
  • Lululemon Athletica (LULU) – China exposure: 10%
  • Agilent Technologies (A) – China exposure: 20%
  • Otis Worldwide Corp. (OTIS) – China exposure: 17%
  • MGM Resorts Int. (MGM) – China exposure: 20%
  • Apple (AAPL) – China exposure: 19%
  • Revvity (RVTY) – China exposure: 17%
  • Viatris (VTRS) – China exposure: 14%
  • Western Digital (WDC) – China exposure: 23%
  • Ebay (EBAY) – China exposure: 10%

data.title: M&A intrigue: Soho House trades 18% below its takeover offer price
data.description:
Soho House & Co (NYSE:SHCO) is being sized up by investors, a day after the company confirmed it received an acquisition offer at $9.00 per share from a third-party consortium. The offer is conditioned on certain significant shareholders, including Executive Chairman Ron Burkle, and The Yucaipa Companies, rolling over their equity interests in the company as part of the transaction.

Roth MKM kept a Buy rating on Soho House (SHCO). Analyst George Kelly said with the stock closing on Thursday well below the $9 offer, the market is clearly skeptical of the buyout deal due to a lack of details in respect to the consortium makeup and the additional conditional shareholders, who too were not made public. "We wonder if the consortium is associated with Burkle or Yucaipa, or if they may not be well received by SHCO's member base," Kelly asked.

Kelly thinks SHCO is an attractive asset and that a transaction is likely down the road with "motivated" large shareholders in the mix.

Of note, Soho House's (SHCO) board turned down an offer last year from an unspecified bidder.

Soho House (SHCO) traded 5.7% higher on Friday after a huge rally on Thursday, Shares are swapping hands at $7.63, which is about 18% below the deal price.

data.title: J.P. Morgan’s top Technology stocks for 2025
data.description:
J.P. Morgan’s U.S. equity analysts picked their top stocks for 2025, selecting “the most compelling investment ideas” across every sector.

These are the technology stocks (NYSEARCA:XLK) highlighted:

Aramark (ARMK) – Price target 45.00

J.P. Morgan analysts said they have “been impressed by Aramark’s (ARMK) execution, absent two large contract losses in 2024 that we view as highly idiosyncratic.”

They expect the company’s base revenues to continue to benefit from increasing automation and self-checkout penetration. “These technologies help drive transaction volumes and allow for a wider array of service formats.”

The company “also possesses clear paths ahead for both margin expansion and financial deleveraging… [trading] at 20x 2025E EPS, versus 25x for best-in-class peer Compass Group (OTCPK:CMPGF).”

Broadcom (AVGO) – Price target: 250.00

“The AI infrastructure build-out driving is strong demand for Broadcom’s (AVGO) ASIC (application-specific integrated circuit)/networking solutions,” analysts said.

AI revenues this year were $12.2B, up 3x year-over-year. “We believe [Broadcom is] set to grow to $17-18B (up 40% year-over-year) in 2025 given continued strong cloud/hyperscaler capex spending trends with focus on AI infrastructure build-out and ramp of Google’s next-gen TPU v6 3nm AI accelerator ASIC.”

CyberArk (CYBR) – Price target: 375.00

“While the company continues to expand outside its core PAM (potential available markets), CyberArk (CYBR) remains one of the best-positioned vendors within our coverage with an improving growth profile and expanding margins,” analysts said.

Dell Technologies (DELL) – Price target: 160.00

“We anticipate a broadening of demand drivers to include Traditional IT Infrastructure in 2025, moving beyond the focused investments in AI seen in 2024, which situates Dell (DELL) as one of the best-positioned companies within our coverage, due to materially stronger core business growth.”

Analysts estimate the earnings potential from Traditional Infrastructure demand, assuming a normal spending environment, to be about $9 per share and project about $2 per share of earnings potential from AI Infrastructure “which supports a bull-case valuation that is materially higher than the current share price.”

Guidewire Software (GWRE) – Price target: 228.00

“Guidewire (GWRE) holds a dominant competitive position among larger insurers, with customers highlighting its robust product roadmap and strong execution as key advantages, enabling the company to command premium pricing,” analysts said.

The company is “well-positioned for accelerated growth heading into 2025E.”

“Management is increasingly confident that subscription and support gross margins could eventually exceed 80%,” J.P. Morgan added. “While Guidewire (GWRE) may maintain substantial investments in its platform, product development, and third-party partnerships, which could limit operating leverage, we believe the company is well-positioned for long-term growth and profitability improvements.”

HubSpot (HUBS) – Price target: 635.00

“Any improvement in the small business environment and/or resumption of front-office investments could serve as positive developments for HubSpot (HUBS), while it is also benefiting from a price increase that is going into effect.”

KLA Corp. (KLAC) – Price target: 950.00

Analysts said that “major current and new technology inflections, combined with new product introductions, Actinic mask inspection, new metrology tools for 3D structures, and enhanced software solutions should drive a strong, multi-year runway of demand for KLA’s process control, packaging, and specialty semiconductor systems.”

Joint Stock Co. Kaspi.kz (OTC:KSPI) – Price target: 137.00

“Fundamentals and Kaspi’s competitive advantages remain as strong as ever, and we think there is low-hanging fruit on the investor messaging side to improve sentiment,” analysts said.

Shares trade 8x 2025 earnings despite a greater than 20% revenue growth and a greater than 40% net income margins.

Mastercard (MA) – Price target: 590.00

“Already-identified pricing should support stable 2025 growth with upside if Mastercard (MA) implements further tokenization pricing (particularly in U.S. market),” analysts said. “We see absolute valuation upside for both networks, particularly Visa (V) as it has a ceiling to raise tokenization pricing, which could create a rising tide to both network’s multiples.”

Mondays.com (MNDY) – Price target: 350.00

“The company has executed well despite high expectations from investors, driving a 34% year-over-year last-twelve-month revenue growth rate, at a more than $1B revenue rate, while producing a 31% free cash flow margin, despite a choppy macro backdrop.”

In addition, analyst believe that “the company presents a unique opportunity as it breaks out of the mold of collaborative work management into a multi-product story, with an edge to enable low-code, no-code business workflow orchestration, while driving a strong profitable growth glide path at a considerable scale.”

Synopsys Inc. (SNPS) – Price target: 685.00

“We believe the company’s recent 2025 guidance includes several areas of conservatism/de-risking and sets up for potential upward revisions.”

Also, “semiconductor chip design activity continues to be strong and driving incremental demand for more/better chip design software/hardware tools,” analysts said. “We continue to see the team driving low-double-digit percentage revenue CAGR over the next several years.”

TransUnion (TRU) – Price target: 124.00

“The valuation on TransUnion (TRU) shares at 24x 2025 EPS remains below peer Equifax (EFX) at 33x and the information services sector median of 30x,” J.P. Morgan analysts said.

“Historically, TransUnion (TRU) had traded above Equifax (EFX) and the sector’s median given [its] higher organic, constant currency revenue growth profile… We think the stock will continue to re-rate higher as long as the team continues to deliver against expectations.”
